question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user permanently exhaust the shared gasLimitBucket by sending a flood of POST /api/v1/contracts/call requests each with gas=15000000 (the default maxGasLimit), given that throttleManager.throttle(request) consumes tokens before validateContractMaxGasLimit() runs, causing all subsequent legitimate requests to receive HTTP 429 and the network's web3 layer to stop processing new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the rateLimitBucket (default 500 RPS) by sending 501+ concurrent POST /api/v1/contracts/call requests per second from a single IP or distributed source, given there is no per-IP rate limiting in the controller, causing all legitimate callers to be throttled and the endpoint to become unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause permanent gas token drain by crafting a request where @Valid passes but Address.fromHexString(request.getFrom()) throws an unchecked IllegalArgumentException inside constructServiceParameters(), given that the catch block only catches InvalidParametersException and therefore throttleManager.restore() is never called, permanently consuming gas tokens from the shared gasLimitBucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause permanent gas token drain by crafting a request where @Valid passes but Address.fromHexString(request.getTo()) throws an unchecked exception inside constructServiceParameters(), given that only InvalidParametersException triggers throttleManager.restore(request.getGas()), causing the gasLimitBucket to be permanently depleted over repeated requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user amplify resource consumption 20x by setting estimate=true in every POST /api/v1/contracts/call request, given that processCall() triggers estimateGas() which invokes BinaryGasEstimator.search() with up to maxGasEstimateRetriesCount=20 EVM executions per request, causing CPU and thread pool exhaustion that prevents the service from processing new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the gasLimitBucket by sending requests with gas exactly equal to evmProperties.getMaxGasLimit() (15,000,000) without triggering validateContractMaxGasLimit() rejection (since the check is strictly greater-than), consuming the maximum possible tokens per request and starving all other callers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service by sending requests with the maximum allowed data field size (262,146 hex characters = 131,072 bytes) combined with estimate=true, given that hexToBytes(data) allocates a large byte array and BinaryGasEstimator runs up to 20 iterations each passing this large callData to the EVM, exhausting heap memory and causing OOM or GC pressure that halts transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: validateContractMaxGasLimit()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass gas throttle token restoration by sending a request with gas > evmProperties.getMaxGasLimit(), given that throttleManager.throttle(request) runs first and consumes tokens, then validateContractMaxGasLimit() throws InvalidParametersException which triggers throttleManager.restore(request.getGas()), but if the restore logic has an off-by-one or scaling mismatch the tokens may not be fully restored, gradually draining the gasLimitBucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause thread pool exhaustion by sending many concurrent requests with estimate=true targeting a computationally expensive contract, given that each request holds a servlet thread for the full duration of up to 20 BinaryGasEstimator iterations, and the default requestTimeout=10000ms allows each thread to be held for up to 10 seconds, starving the thread pool and preventing new transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of throttleManager.throttle(request) before validateContractMaxGasLimit(request) to perform a gas token exhaustion attack where they send gas=Long.MAX_VALUE (bypassing @Min(21000) but exceeding maxGasLimit), causing throttle() to attempt to consume a massive scaled token count that overflows or saturates the gasLimitBucket before the validation rejects the request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained denial of service by exploiting the fact that the gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED, sending a high volume of concurrent requests that all contend on the same lock, causing thread contention and latency spikes that prevent the service from accepting new transactions?
